title: DHS OIG Announces 8 Simultaneous Reviews of ICE Enforcement and Training
excerpt: The DHS Office of Inspector General announced eight concurrent oversight reviews covering ICE hiring and training capacity, use-of-force investigations, 287(g) compliance, and procedures for preventing the arrest of U.S. citizens — the broadest single OIG oversight action in the agency's history.
content:
On February 5, 2026, the DHS Office of Inspector General publicly announced it was conducting eight simultaneous oversight reviews of DHS immigration enforcement programs, citing the surge in reported incidents involving ICE and CBP agents.

  • ICE's Hiring and Training Program — to determine whether ICE can surge hiring and training to meet operational needs.
  • ICE's Oversight of the 287(g) Program — to assess whether partner agencies comply with program requirements.
  • ICE's Processes for Investigating Excessive Force Allegations — to determine whether ICE holds personnel accountable under applicable law and DHS policy.
  • DHS Processes for Preventing the Arrest of U.S. Citizens — in direct response to documented cases of citizens detained during immigration operations.
  • Additional reviews covered expedited removal authority, CBP interior enforcement, unannounced detention facility inspections, and biometric data management.

The OIG did not provide a completion timeline. The announcement came amid growing congressional pressure following the fatal shootings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti in Minneapolis and mounting excessive-force claims documented in federal tort filings.

OIG noted it would immediately alert DHS leadership and Congress if investigators identify issues posing a serious or imminent threat to safety, health, property, or continuity of operations.
